assessment_items.question_content:
**There are visual and thematic connections between the scenes on the left and right doors.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/99c35fe5a15c6ad564c0bd29bbee99bd.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**This is correct. There are visual and thematic connections between the scenes on the left and right doors. In the image, there are visual and thematic connections between The Fall on the left and The Crucifixion on the right.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/ottonian/v/saint-michaels-hildesheim)**

assessment_items.question_content: **The doors show scenes from [[☃ dropdown 1]] on the left and scenes from the life of [[☃ dropdown 2]] on the right.**
hints:
**The doors show scenes from Genesis on the left and scenes from the life of Christ on the right.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/ottonian/v/saint-michaels-hildesheim)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Each of the doors was cast as a single piece using the lost wax method, revived from antiquity.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4cf5e3e098cc246618045d18f2e32922.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**This is correct. Each of the doors was cast as a single piece, which was unusual for this time.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/ottonian/v/saint-michaels-hildesheim)**

assessment_items.question_content: **Bishop Bernward saw monumental doors at Santa Sabina in [[☃ dropdown 1]] and wanted similar monumental doors in Hildesheim.**
hints:
**Bishop Bernward saw the monumental wooden doors at Santa Sabina in Rome and wanted similar monumental doors in Hildesheim.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/ottonian/v/saint-michaels-hildesheim)**
